skill.name: human-docs
skill.description: Transforms detailed AI-facing documentation into concise human documentation, including summaries, quick starts, and Mermaid visual guides.
skill.summary: Create concise summaries, quick starts, and diagrams from detailed AI documentation.
skill.author: Brownbull

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are false positives from Markdown fences, Mermaid diagram syntax, and documentation examples. No malicious intent, credential access, command execution, prompt injection, or data exfiltration was found. The skill does instruct agents to read AI docs and write generated files under docs/, so filesystem behavior should remain clear to users.

low_findings.title: False-positive command execution flags in Markdown examples
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby or shell backtick findings are Markdown code fences, inline code, Mermaid blocks, and command placeholders. They are documentation examples, not executable skill code, and no dynamic command execution path was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line-numbered review shows the flagged backticks are fenced Markdown and Mermaid syntax. No script file or runtime command invocation is present in these locations.

low_findings.title: False-positive malware keyword and weak-crypto flags
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography, C2, and reconnaissance alerts match benign documentation terms, file extensions, Mermaid identifiers, or project labels. No hashing, encryption, beaconing, system probing, or command-and-control behavior was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious terms occur inside prose, filenames, and Mermaid diagram labels. Semantic review found no code path using cryptography or remote-control behavior.

low_findings.title: External documentation links are present
low_findings.description: The skill includes public Mermaid documentation and live-editor URLs. These links are visible documentation references and do not send data automatically.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are explicit user-facing references to Mermaid resources. There is no network client, hidden request, or exfiltration mechanism in the reviewed files.

low_findings.title: Generated documentation writes to the project workspace
low_findings.description: The skill directs agents to save generated summaries, quick starts, diagrams, and indexes under docs/. This is expected for a documentation generator, but users should review generated files before committing them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The write behavior is stated directly in the workflow instructions. It is legitimate for this skill, but it changes files in the user workspace.

content.value_statement: Detailed AI documentation can be too dense for quick human review. This skill turns source docs into summaries, quick starts, and Mermaid visual guides.
actual_capabilities: Creates concise summary documents from detailed AI documentation.
Builds quick start guides with prerequisites, steps, checks, and troubleshooting.
Creates Mermaid visual guides for architecture, data flow, process, and schema topics.
Keeps a parallel docs/ structure that mirrors the ai/ source structure.
Updates existing human docs when source AI docs change.
Generates a docs index for batch documentation work.
limitations: Requires existing source documentation or a clear topic description.
Does not replace the ai/ folder as the source of truth.
Generated docs still need human review for accuracy and tone.
Mermaid diagrams depend on markdown viewers that support Mermaid rendering.

use_cases.title: Onboard a new engineer
use_cases.description: Turn architecture docs into a short reading path with summaries, diagrams, and task-focused quick starts.

use_cases.title: Prepare an executive review
use_cases.description: Convert technical specifications into brief summaries and visual system overviews for non-technical stakeholders.

use_cases.title: Create developer reference docs
use_cases.description: Generate quick references from API, workflow, or implementation documents for daily engineering use.

prompt_templates.title: Create a basic summary
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one source document.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a human-friendly summary for ai/architect/app/02_BACKEND_REQUIREMENTS.md and save it under the matching docs path.

prompt_templates.title: Build a quick start
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a five-minute quick start from ai/architect/app/05_CSV_UPLOAD_SPECIFICATION.md with prerequisites, steps, checks, and troubleshooting.

prompt_templates.title: Generate visual diagrams
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for visual system understanding.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create Mermaid diagrams for ai/architect/app/07_WORKFLOWS_AND_DATA_FLOW.md, including overview, data flow, and process diagrams.

prompt_templates.title: Refresh a documentation set
prompt_templates.prompt: Review ai/architect/app/, update matching human docs in docs/architect/app/, preserve manual notes, and regenerate the docs index.

output_examples.input: A long backend requirements document for an internal application.
output_examples.output: A concise summary with an executive overview, five key points, a quick reference table, and links to full details.

output_examples.input: A detailed CSV upload specification for developers.
output_examples.output: A quick start guide with prerequisites, setup steps, verification checks, common issues, and next steps.

output_examples.input: A workflow document describing data movement between services.
output_examples.output: A visual guide with Mermaid overview, sequence, process, and data-flow diagrams plus short explanations.

best_practices: Keep the ai/ folder as the source of truth and generate human docs from it.
Review generated summaries and diagrams before sharing them with stakeholders.
Use summaries for decisions, quick starts for tasks, and diagrams for complex systems.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill to create source documentation from nothing.
Do not manually edit generated docs without updating the source AI documentation.
Do not include every detail from the source document in a human summary.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates summaries, quick start guides, Mermaid visual guides, and documentation indexes from detailed AI docs.

faq.question: Does it edit the source AI documentation?
faq.answer: No. The skill says the ai/ folder remains the source of truth and generated docs belong under docs/.

faq.question: Can it process a folder of documents?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a batch workflow for generating matching human docs and an index README.

faq.question: Does it require Mermaid support?
faq.answer: Mermaid support is needed to render diagrams directly in markdown viewers. The diagram source remains editable text.

faq.question: Is this useful for non-technical readers?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create executive summaries and visual guides with reduced technical detail.

faq.question: Should generated docs be reviewed?
faq.answer: Yes. A human should verify accuracy, scope, links, and diagram clarity before publishing.
